methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Distalmotion is a Series G robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine private companies in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, alongside one public comparable (277810.KQ).

Given the nascent state of direct peer data at Distalmotion's stage and valuation tier, the primary applicable method is stage-progression analysis. Using the $150M cumulative capital raised as a valuation proxy, a 1.25x step-up multiplier—consistent with typical Series G to subsequent-stage progression—yields an implied valuation of $188M. This method anchors the analysis in observable funding history rather than sparse late-stage peer multiples.

A peer-median approach was deemed inapplicable due to insufficient comparable density. While 11 peers operate in the robotics and manufacturing sector, fewer than three qualify as same-stage or adjacent-stage matches with similar valuation tiers, falling short of the minimum threshold for reliable benchmarking.

The triangulated valuation range is $159M (low) to $216M (high), with a median of $188M. This narrow bandwidth reflects reliance on a single robust method rather than consensus across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-peer comparables at Series G scale and the sensitivity of the stage-progression multiplier to market conditions and capital availability in frontier robotics. Validation through revenue multiples or customer-concentration metrics would strengthen conviction.
